entries.statement: Ship a lightweight health-check and model status strip—validate endpoint, confirm model availability, run one short generation smoke test, and explain failures in plain language—before investing in a full wizard.

basis.intent: Reduce setup confusion and opaque failure states
supporting_evidence: Three support threads in two weeks involved local setup confusion
An internal demo failed because the required model was not pulled
Engineering estimates endpoint validation as straightforward and smoke test as medium effort
assumptions_relied_on: Most setup failures are endpoint, model, or port mismatches rather than Capture Layer logic bugs
A short generation smoke test is more trustworthy than endpoint ping alone
risks_addressed: Health checks may produce false positives or false negatives
risks_accepted: Maintenance burden if local runtime APIs change frequently
constraints_respected: Mock demo remains the public default; local runtime is not exposed in visitor UI
Engineering has limited capacity before the next Capture Layer quality push
tradeoffs: Design wants a fast workflow; support wants explicit readiness signals
alternatives_considered: Build a guided first-run wizard with install checks, model pull confirmation, and plain-language failures
Improve documentation and troubleshooting only, with no in-product flow
missing_context_caveats: Engineering estimate for status strip vs wizard

would_change_if: If support volume for setup failures does not drop after the status strip ships.
If engineering estimates a status strip at wizard-level effort once scoped.

entries.statement: Scope a one-sprint status strip with endpoint, model, and smoke-test checks

basis.intent: Avoid pulling engineering away from Capture Layer quality work
constraints_respected: Engineering has limited capacity before the next Capture Layer quality push
missing_context_caveats: Engineering estimate for status strip vs wizard

would_change_if: If engineering scoping reveals the status strip needs more than one sprint.

entries.statement: Define plain-language failure copy for the top three setup failure modes

basis.intent: Reduce setup confusion and opaque failure states
supporting_evidence: A documentation update helped one user but not a port-mismatch case
missing_context_caveats: Support ticket taxonomy for setup vs generation failures

would_change_if: If the support ticket taxonomy shows different top failure modes than assumed.

entries.statement: Measure support volume for four weeks after release

basis.intent: Reduce setup confusion and opaque failure states
supporting_evidence: Three support threads in two weeks involved local setup confusion
missing_context_caveats: Quantified local vs mock usage among active users

would_change_if: If support volume does not measurably drop within the four-week window.

entries.statement: Revisit a guided wizard only if setup-related tickets remain high

basis.intent: Make the local-first generation path credible for power users
risks_accepted: Scope could creep into model management and broad local-stack support
alternatives_considered: Build a guided first-run wizard with install checks, model pull confirmation, and plain-language failures

would_change_if: If setup-related tickets remain high after the status strip and failure-copy updates ship.
